# n-dx

> Codebase analysis generated by Sourcevision.
> When using this document: group related items together and separate unrelated concerns.

## Project Overview

- **Files**: 554
- **Lines of code**: 159,466
- **Languages**: TypeScript (448), CSS (27), JSON (26), Other (20), JavaScript (15)
- **Zones**: 9 (9 with descriptions)
- **Git**: feature/from-recommend @ a67e98f
- **Analyzed**: 2026-02-11T02:58:24.881Z

## Architecture Zones

### Autonomous Agent Engine (`autonomous-agent-engine`)

AI-powered agent execution layer that orchestrates task completion through controlled coupling with Rex for legitimate task management operations.

- Files: 100
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/autonomous-agent-engine/context.md`

### Claude Integration Layer (`claude-integration-layer`)

Foundation layer providing dependency inversion for Claude API access, authentication abstractions, and shared types that prevent circular dependencies.

- Files: 18
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/claude-integration-layer/context.md`

### Integrated Web Dashboard (`integrated-web-dashboard`)

Full-stack web application that integrates all domain packages through filesystem-driven data consumption and provides unified dashboard with MCP server endpoints.

- Files: 137
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/integrated-web-dashboard/context.md`

### Orchestration Layer (`orchestration-layer`)

Pure coordination layer that delegates to package CLIs through process spawning while maintaining strict architectural isolation.

- Files: 4
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/orchestration-layer/context.md`

### Rex PRD Management CLI (`packages-rex:rex-cli`)

A self-contained CLI tool and MCP server for managing hierarchical product requirements documents, including code analysis, tree operations, validation, persistence, and AI-agent workflow integration.

- Files: 36
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/packages-rex:rex-cli/context.md`

### Analysis Engine (`packages-sourcevision:analysis-engine`)

Core analysis pipeline including file inventory, import graph extraction, Louvain community detection, route parsing, AI enrichment, and CLI orchestration.

- Files: 33
- Cohesion: 0.94, Coupling: 0.06
- Entry points: packages/sourcevision/src/analyzers/components.ts, packages/sourcevision/src/analyzers/imports.ts, packages/sourcevision/src/analyzers/inventory.ts, packages/sourcevision/src/analyzers/zones.ts, packages/sourcevision/src/schema/data-files.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/packages-sourcevision:analysis-engine/context.md`

### Interactive Viewer (`packages-sourcevision:interactive-viewer`)

Preact single-page application that visualizes analysis results through force-directed graphs, zone maps, route trees, and findings panels, consuming only schema types with zero analyzer dependencies.

- Files: 46
- Cohesion: 0.96, Coupling: 0.04
- Entry points: packages/sourcevision/src/schema/v1.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/packages-sourcevision:interactive-viewer/context.md`

### Test Fixtures (`packages-sourcevision:test-fixtures`)

Self-contained sample TypeScript project used as input data for integration and end-to-end tests, intentionally isolated with no imports from or exports to production code.

- Files: 7
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/packages-sourcevision:test-fixtures/context.md`

### Test Suite (`packages-sourcevision:test-suite`)

Unit, integration, and end-to-end tests covering analyzer logic, schema validation, CLI workflows, and the serve command, with imports reaching into both analyzer and viewer zones.

- Files: 5
- Cohesion: 0.46, Coupling: 0.54
- Entry points: packages/sourcevision/src/schema/validate.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/packages-sourcevision:test-suite/context.md`

## Route Structure

### Route Tree

```
/ → packages/sourcevision/tests/fixtures/remix-app/app/routes/_index.tsx
/login → packages/sourcevision/tests/fixtures/remix-app/app/routes/_auth.login.tsx
/users/:id → packages/sourcevision/tests/fixtures/remix-app/app/routes/users.$id.tsx
```

### Convention Exports

- action: 2 modules
- default: 4 modules
- loader: 2 modules
- meta: 1 modules

### Components: 5 total, 0 usage edges

## Findings

### Patterns

- Gateway pattern usage consistently isolates cross-package imports into auditable single modules [info]
- Layered architecture with strict unidirectional dependencies creates clean separation of concerns across all tiers [info]
- Mixed delegation strategies (process-based at orchestration, library-based at execution) optimize for different architectural constraints [info]

### Relationships

- Zero circular dependencies across 308 files and 4 zones demonstrates exceptional architectural discipline [info]

### Observations

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (autonomous-agent-engine)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (claude-integration-layer)
- Size imbalance: largest zone has 137 files vs smallest with 4 — uneven decomposition [info]
- Contains 44% of project files (137/308) — subdivided into 7 sub-zones [info] (integrated-web-dashboard)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (integrated-web-dashboard)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (orchestration-layer)
- All zones maintain perfect cohesion (1.0) and zero inter-zone coupling, indicating excellent architectural boundaries [info]

### Suggestions

- Large test suite structure across unit/integration/e2e could benefit from test utility consolidation to reduce maintenance overhead [info] (autonomous-agent-engine)
- Process execution abstraction promotion to foundation layer successfully eliminated code duplication but should be consistently adopted across all packages [info] (claude-integration-layer)
- Consider implementing automated detection for direct process execution imports that bypass foundation layer abstractions [info]
- Multiple viewer sub-zones across the codebase suggest need for UI component lifecycle and consolidation governance [warning]
- Direct child_process imports in routes-rex.ts should be refactored to use foundation layer spawnTool abstraction for architectural consistency [warning] (integrated-web-dashboard)
- Multiple viewer sub-zones suggest need for viewer consolidation strategy to prevent UI component fragmentation and maintenance burden [warning] (integrated-web-dashboard)
- Consider standardizing command delegation pattern - some commands spawn CLIs directly while others may benefit from shared argument parsing/validation [info] (orchestration-layer)
